# lfreleng-actions: globally allow-listed Python audit vulnerabilities
#
# Format: whitespace-separated vulnerability IDs. '#' introduces a
# comment, either as a full-line comment or trailing on a non-comment
# line. Blank lines are ignored. Each entry should reference an
# upstream issue or note the reason it appears here.
#
# Consumed by: https://github.com/lfreleng-actions/python-audit-action

# pip GHSA-4xh5-x5gv-qwph (CVE-2025-8869): pip's fallback tar
# extraction does not check that symlinks resolve inside the
# extraction directory. Fixed upstream in pip 25.3 (Sept 2025), but
# actions/setup-python images often still ship pip <= 25.2; remove
# this entry once the runner-shipped pip reliably ships >= 25.3.
# https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-4xh5-x5gv-qwph
GHSA-4xh5-x5gv-qwph

# pyjwt PYSEC-2025-183 (CVE-2025-45768): "weak encryption" claim
# disputed by upstream. The key length is the responsibility of the
# application that uses the library, not pyjwt itself. No fix is
# planned; this entry is permanent.
# https://osv.dev/vulnerability/PYSEC-2025-183
PYSEC-2025-183
